blog_posts.title: Winter Parking Lot Maintenance Checklist for Property Managers
blog_posts.summary: A step-by-step winter asphalt maintenance checklist to keep commercial parking lots safe, clear, and damage-free all season.
blog_posts.content: Preparing your parking lot for winter requires a structured approach. This checklist walks property managers through pre-season inspections, crack sealing, pothole repairs, de-icing strategies, snow piling locations, and post-storm inspections. Key steps include: 1) Inspect and seal cracks to prevent water intrusion and freeze–thaw damage. 2) Repair potholes and depressions before temperatures drop. 3) Confirm your snow removal plan, including trigger depths and priority areas. 4) Mark fire lanes, ADA stalls, and pedestrian routes with high-visibility striping. 5) Establish a de-icer plan that minimizes surface damage. Use this checklist before the first freeze and repeat quick inspections after each major storm.

blog_posts.title: Residential Driveway Winter Prep Checklist
blog_posts.summary: Homeowner-friendly checklist to prepare your asphalt driveway for freezing temperatures and snow.
blog_posts.content: Use this residential checklist each fall to protect your asphalt driveway from winter damage. Start by sweeping and power washing, then fill cracks and small potholes. Consider a fall sealcoat if the surface is due. Mark edges and obstacles before the first snowfall to protect landscaping and curbs during plowing. Avoid metal shovels, and choose de-icers that are safe for asphalt surfaces. Finally, schedule a spring inspection to catch any damage early.

blog_posts.title: 7 Winter Asphalt Care Mistakes to Avoid in Your Parking Lot
blog_posts.summary: Common winter asphalt maintenance mistakes that lead to premature cracking, potholes, and safety issues.
blog_posts.content: Cold weather amplifies any existing weaknesses in your asphalt. From skipping crack sealing to using the wrong de-icing products, this article highlights the most common winter asphalt care mistakes. Learn why proper drainage, timely snow removal, and proactive repairs make the difference between a smooth lot and one full of potholes by spring.

blog_posts.title: Summer Sealcoating Guide for Commercial Parking Lots
blog_posts.summary: How to plan and schedule summer sealcoating for minimal downtime and maximum pavement life.
blog_posts.content: Summer offers ideal conditions for sealcoating commercial parking lots. This guide covers surface preparation, curing times, traffic control planning, and how to coordinate sealcoating around your busiest business hours. We also explain how to budget by square foot and how often to reapply sealer based on traffic levels.

blog_posts.title: Asphalt Repair vs. Replacement: How to Decide
blog_posts.summary: Learn whether crack sealing, patching, overlays, or full replacement is the best option for your pavement.
blog_posts.content: Not every damaged lot needs a full replacement. This article explains the decision process between crack sealing, pothole patching, resurfacing (overlay), and full-depth reconstruction. We outline condition thresholds, typical costs, and how an on-site evaluation helps you choose the right scope for your budget.

blog_posts.title: Understanding Mill-and-Pave Estimates for 10,000+ Sq Ft Lots
blog_posts.summary: Breakdown of how mill-and-pave projects are estimated by square foot, depth, and add-ons like striping.
blog_posts.content: Mill-and-pave projects are typically priced by square foot and depth of asphalt. This article reviews common depth options (1.5", 2", 3"), how sub-base repairs affect pricing, and why add-ons like line striping and curb repair are often listed separately. We walk through a sample 12,000 sq ft parking lot estimate so you know what to expect.

blog_posts.title: Choosing the Right Commercial Repair Package for Cracks and Potholes
blog_posts.summary: How to select a commercial repair package that includes both crack sealing and pothole patching within your budget.
blog_posts.content: Crack sealing and pothole patching often go hand-in-hand. We explain why combining these services in a single package improves pavement performance and simplifies budgeting. Learn which package tiers to look for if you're targeting repairs under $5,000 and how to compare feature lists.

blog_posts.title: Snow Removal Best Practices for Commercial Parking Lots
blog_posts.summary: Learn how to structure a snow removal plan that protects your asphalt and keeps tenants safe.
blog_posts.content: An effective snow removal plan includes defined trigger depths, clear plow routes, designated snow piling areas, and communication protocols. We discuss how to pair snow removal with ongoing maintenance plans so your lots stay safe and your asphalt lasts longer.

blog_posts.title: Emergency Asphalt Repair: What to Do After Hours
blog_posts.summary: Steps to take when a pothole, sinkhole, or heaved area appears in your lot outside normal business hours.
blog_posts.content: When asphalt failures happen after hours, safety is the priority. This article outlines how to cordon off hazards, document damage, and contact a 24/7 emergency repair service. We describe the information to include on an emergency request form so crews arrive prepared.

blog_posts.title: Annual Asphalt Maintenance Calendar for Property Managers
blog_posts.summary: Month-by-month guide to inspections, repairs, sealcoating, and snow planning.
blog_posts.content: From spring inspections to fall crack sealing and winter snow planning, this calendar shows what to schedule each season. Using a maintenance plan helps spread costs evenly and prevents surprise failures.

blog_posts.title: Line Striping and ADA Compliance Guide
blog_posts.summary: Understand ADA requirements for accessible parking stalls, signage, and markings.
blog_posts.content: Proper line striping is critical for ADA compliance. This guide explains stall dimensions, access aisles, signage, and marking colors, and how often to re-stripe to maintain visibility.

service_types.description: Milling off the top layer of asphalt and installing new pavement to a specified depth.
